crosshair_version_source: pip show crosshair-tool -> 'Name: crosshair-tool' / 'Version: 0.0.107' (crosshair CLI 0.0.107 has no --version flag)
invocation_source: run_manifest_naive_widening.json 'command' field; no bounds flags passed, CrossHair defaults in effect
mechanism_attribution: Class (2) model-fidelity incompleteness, not search-budget. CrossHair 0.0.107 predominantly models Python floats as mathematical reals; IEEE-faithful floating-point execution is attempted only infrequently and is often slow (changelog v0.0.72). In real arithmetic 1e308 * -2.0 = -2e308 exactly - no overflow, no -inf - so the violating state does not exist in the dominant symbolic model at any bounds setting. CrossHair injects nan/+inf/-inf as float ARGUMENTS (changelog v0.0.58); this exhibit's -inf is a computed intermediate from finite inputs, which a real-valued model cannot produce. Changelog: https://crosshair.readthedocs.io/en/stable/changelog.html (v0.0.72, v0.0.58).
version_contingency: The miss is version-contingent: a future CrossHair release that samples floating-point theory more aggressively may confirm the violation. This exhibit's claim is scoped to the pinned version above.
